trial_title: A Phase III, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Multicenter Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of BRC-209 (Velagostat) in Early Alzheimer's Disease
summary: This is a synopsis document for a Phase III, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multicenter trial evaluating BRC-209 (Velagostat) 400 mg daily in early Alzheimer's disease patients. The document provides a high-level overview including the primary endpoint (CDR-SB change at Week 78), key inclusion criteria, schedule of assessments, and statistical analysis plan for 480 patients randomized 2:1 across Spain, UK, Sweden, Netherlands, and Canada.
content:
CONFIDENTIAL — FOR AUTHORISED PERSONNEL ONLY




BIORCE 
THERAPEUTICS


CLINICAL STUDY PROTOCOL


A Phase III, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Multicenter Study
to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of BRC-209 (Velagostat) in Early Alzheimer's Disease




Protocol Number
BIORCE-NEU-2022-004


Version / Date
1.0 — 03 June 2022


Investigational Product
BRC-209 (Velagostat) 400 mg QD


Indication
Early Alzheimer's Disease


Sponsor
Biorce Therapeutics S.L., Madrid


EudraCT
2022-003891-28


Phase
Phase III


Target Enrollment
480 patients (2:1)






1. SYNOPSIS




Parameter
Details


Primary Endpoint
Change from baseline in CDR-SB at Week 78


Key Secondary Endpoints
ADAS-Cog13, ADCS-ADL-MCI, MMSE, amyloid PET, tau PET, safety


Treatment Duration
96 weeks + 24-week follow-up


Countries
Spain, UK, Sweden, Netherlands, Canada




2. INCLUSION CRITERIA




Age 50–80 years


MCI due to AD or mild AD dementia (NIA-AA 2018)


Positive amyloid PET (Centiloid ≥25) or CSF Aβ42/40 <0.10


CDR Global Score 0.5 or 1.0


MMSE score 20–30


Stable AD medications ≥3 months


Reliable study partner ≥10h/week


Brain MRI within 6 months — no significant abnormality




3. SCHEDULE OF ASSESSMENTS (abbreviated)




Assessment
Wk0
Wk13
Wk26
Wk52
Wk78
EOT


CDR-SB
✓
✓
✓
✓
✓
✓


ADAS-Cog13
✓
✓
✓
✓
✓
✓


Amyloid PET
✓
✓


Tau PET
✓
✓


Blood (haem/chem)
✓
✓
✓
✓
✓
✓


Study Drug
✓
✓
✓
✓
✓




4. STATISTICAL ANALYSIS


480 patients (2:1) provide 90% power to detect 0.7-point CDR-SB difference at Week 78
(SD 2.5, two-sided α=0.05, 15% dropout). Primary analysis: MMRM with CDR-SB as
dependent variable, treatment, visit, treatment-by-visit, baseline CDR-SB, APOE ε4 status.




BIORCE-NEU-2022-004 | Protocol v1.0 | 03 June 2022


CONFIDENTIAL © 2022 Biorce Therapeutics S.L.
